'''Hollace Davids''' is the co-author of The [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|Jedi Prince]] young-reader series along with her husband [[Paul Davids|Paul]]. Hollace grew up in [[Wikipedia: Silver Spring, Maryland|Silver Spring, Maryland]] and attended [[Wikipedia: Goucher College|Goucher College]] and [[Wikipedia: Boston University|Boston University]]. In [[1971]], she met her future husband, Paul, in [[Wikipedia:Harvard Square|Harvard Square]] in [[Wikipedia: Cambridge, Massachusetts|Cambridge]], after a screening of the [[George Lucas]] film, [[Wikipedia:THX-1138|''THX-1138'']]. The Davids have two children together, Jordan and Scott.

Davids is the Senior Vice President of Special Projects at [[Wikipedia:Universal Pictures|Universal Pictures]] in the Feature Film Marketing Department. She was previously the Vice President of Special Events at [[Wikipedia:Columbia Pictures|Columbia Pictures]] and ran her own marketing and special events consulting agency before joining Universal in [[1995]]. Davids was the President of Women In Film from [[1978]]–[[2000]], and in the latter year she won both the Jim Yeager Award for Public Service from the Publicists Guild and the award for Mother of the Year by the California Mothers Association. Davids is a member of both the [[Wikipedia:Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences|Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] and the [[Wikipedia:Producers Guild of America|Producers Guild of America]].<ref name="Greenlight" />
